markdown:
# Get started with Sanity UI

Sanity UI is a scalable and ergonomic React library for rapidly building efficient, accessible, and beautiful web apps:

```
npm install @sanity/ui

# Install peer dependencies 
npm install react react-dom styled-components
```

[![](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@sanity/ui.svg?style=flat-square)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@sanity/ui)

## Basic usage  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs#basic-usage)  

**Note**: `@sanity/ui` only comes with one theme right now – `studioTheme` – which is built for use in Sanity products.

```
import {
  Card,
  Heading,
  ThemeProvider
} from '@sanity/ui'
import { buildTheme } from '@sanity/ui/theme'
import React from 'react'
import ReactDOM from 'react-dom'

ReactDOM.render(
  <App />,
  document.getElementById('root')
)

const theme = buildTheme()

function App () {
  return (
    <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
      <Card padding={4}>
        <Heading>Welcome to Sanity UI</Heading>
      </Card>
    </ThemeProvider>
  )
}
```

# TextInput

Single line text input.

```
import declarations may only appear at top level of a module
```

<Card padding\={4}\>

<TextInput

fontSize\={\[2, 2, 3, 4\]}

onChange\={(event) =>

setValue(event.currentTarget.value)

}

padding\={\[3, 3, 4\]}

placeholder\="TextInput"

value\={value}

/>

</Card\>

## Properties  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/primitive/text-input#properties)  

```
border?: boolean
```

```
clearButton?: boolean
```

```
customValidity?: string
```

```
fontSize?: number | number[]
```

```
icon?: React.ComponentType | React.ReactNode
```

```
iconRight?: React.ComponentType | React.ReactNode
```

```
padding?: number | number[]
```

```
paddingX?: number | number[]
```

```
paddingY?: number | number[]
```

```
paddingTop?: number | number[]
```

```
paddingRight?: number | number[]
```

```
paddingBottom?: number | number[]
```

```
paddingLeft?: number | number[]
```

```
prefix?: React.ReactNode
```

```
radius?: number | number[]
```

```
space?: number | number[]
```

```
suffix?: React.ReactNode
```

```
weight?: 'regular' | 'medium' | 'semibold' | 'bold'
```

## Accessibility considerations  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/primitive/text-input#accessibility-considerations)  

When using `TextInput`, assistive technologies like screenreaders and voice input will expect that they have an accessible name. For input fields, this is sometimes also referred to as a label.

The preferred way to add an accessible name is to add a visible label, so that all users can benefit from it. To add a visible label, add an `id` to `TextInput`, and then a `<label>` element with a `for` attribute that corresponds with the input's `id`:

```
<Card padding={4}>
  <label htmlFor="street-address">Street Address</label>
  <TextInput
    value={value}
    id="street-address"
  />
</Card>
```

Alternatively, a hidden label can be added directly onto the input with `aria-label`:

```
<Card padding={4}>
  <TextInput
    value={value}
    aria-label="Street Address"
  />
</Card>
```

For more information, see:

# Tooltip

Tooltips display information when hovering, focusing or tapping.

```
import declarations may only appear at top level of a module
```

<Card padding\={4} style\={{textAlign: 'center'}}\>

<Text\>

<Tooltip

content\={

<Box padding\={1}\>

<Text muted size\={1}\>

Tooltip content

</Text\>

</Box\>

}

animate

fallbackPlacements\={\['right', 'left'\]}

placement\="top"

portal

\>

<span style\={{display: 'inline-block'}}\>

Hover here

</span\>

</Tooltip\>

</Text\>

</Card\>

## Properties  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/primitive/tooltip#properties)  

```

```

```
animate?: boolean
```

Whether the `Tooltip` should animate in and out.

```

```

```

```

```
children?: React.ReactElement
```

```
content?: React.ReactNode
```

```
delay?: number | Partial<{ open: number; close: number }>
```

An optional delay to apply to the tooltip. If a number is applied, it will be used for both opening and closing.

```
disabled?: boolean
```

```
fallbackPlacements?: Placement[]
```

```
placement?: Placement
```

```
portal?: boolean
```

Whether or not to render the tooltip in a portal element.

```
shadow?: number | number[]
```

# Dialog

The `Dialog` component.

## Basic example  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/component/dialog#basic-example)  

The `Dialog` component is typically rendered conditionally. It also needs an `onClose` property to function as expected:

```
import {useCallback, useState} from 'react'

const [open, setOpen] = useState(false)
const onClose = useCallback(() => setOpen(false), [])
const onOpen = useCallback(() => setOpen(true), [])
```

With that in place, it’s straight-forward to build a basic open/close flow using the `Dialog` component:

```
import declarations may only appear at top level of a module
```

<Card padding\={4} style\={{textAlign: 'center'}}\>

<Button onClick\={onOpen} text\="Open dialog" />

</Card\>

{

open && (

<Dialog

header\="Example"

id\="dialog-example"

onClose\={onClose}

zOffset\={1000}

\>

<Box padding\={4}\>

<Text\>Content</Text\>

</Box\>

</Dialog\>

)

}

## Properties  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/component/dialog#properties)  

```
animate?: boolean
```

Whether the dialog should animate in on mount

```
cardRadius?: number | number[]
```

```
cardShadow?: number | number[]
```

```
footer?: React.ReactNode
```

```
header?: React.ReactNode
```

```
id: string
```

```
onClose?: () => void
```

```
scheme?: 'dark' | 'light'
```

```
width?: number | number[]
```

```
zOffset?: number
```

# Menu

The `Menu` component is a building block for application menus.

## Properties  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/component/menu#properties)  

```

```

When `true`, the last element will be focused on mount.

```
onClickOutside?: () => void
```

```
onEscape?: () => void
```

```
onItemClick?: () => void
```

```
space?: number | number[]
```

## Basic example  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/component/menu#basic-example)  

Hover the menu to see the focused item changing.

```
import declarations may only appear at top level of a module
```

<Box padding\={4}\>

<Card radius\={3} shadow\={2}\>

<Menu\>

<MenuItem

fontSize\={\[2, 2, 3, 4\]}

text\="First option"

/>

<MenuItem

fontSize\={\[2, 2, 3, 4\]}

text\="Second option"

/>

<MenuItem

fontSize\={\[2, 2, 3, 4\]}

text\="Third option"

/>

</Menu\>

</Card\>

</Box\>

```
import declarations may only appear at top level of a module
```

<Box padding\={4}\>

<Card radius\={3} shadow\={2}\>

<Menu\>

<MenuItem\>

<Box padding\={3}\>

<Stack space\={3}\>

<Text muted size\={1}\>

Description

</Text\>

</Stack\>

</MenuItem\>

<MenuItem\>

<Stack space\={3}\>

Description

</Text\>

</Stack\>

</Box\>

</MenuItem\>

<MenuDivider />

<MenuItem tone\="critical"\>

<Box padding\={3}\>

<Stack space\={3}\>

<Text weight\="semibold"\>Dangerous option</Text\>

<Text muted size\={1}\>

Description

</Text\>

</Stack\>

</Box\>

</Menu\>

</Card\>

</Box\>

# Tab

<Card padding\={4}\>

<TabList space\={2}\>

<Tab

aria-controls\="content-panel"

icon\={EditIcon}

id\="content-tab"

label\="Content"

onClick\={() => setId('content')}

selected\={id \=== 'content'}

space\={2}

/>

<Tab

aria-controls\="preview-panel"

icon\={id \=== 'preview' ? EyeOpenIcon : EyeClosedIcon}

id\="preview-tab"

label\="Preview"

onClick\={() => setId('preview')}

selected\={id \=== 'preview'}

space\={2}

/>

</TabList\>

<TabPanel

aria-labelledby\="content-tab"

hidden\={id !== 'content'}

id\="content-panel"

\>

<Card border marginTop\={2} padding\={4} radius\={2}\>

<Heading\>Content</Heading\>

</Card\>

</TabPanel\>

<TabPanel

aria-labelledby\="preview-tab"

hidden\={id !== 'preview'}

id\="preview-panel"

\>

<Card border marginTop\={2} padding\={4}\>

<Heading\>Preview</Heading\>

</Card\>

</TabPanel\>

</Card\>

button\={<Button text\="Open menu" />}

menu\={(

<Menu\>

placement\="right"

/>

# Layer

The `Layer` component is a utility for rendering DOM nodes on top of each other. Each layer are dynamically assigned a `z-index` value.

# Portal | Sanity UI

This is useful for rendering modals, dialogs, popovers, and other components that need to break out of the application – either visually or interactively.

# Why Sanity UI? | Sanity UI

There are **tons** of React UI libraries out there. So why did we build a new one, and why should you care?

## Built for composition  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/motivation#built-for-composition)  

Sanity UI consists mainly of a set of UI primitives. The purpose of these primitives is to provide designers and developers with a set of simple, ergonomic and highly composable building blocks – so they can build more and worry less.

### Typographic primitives  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/motivation#typographic-primitives)  

There are four typographic primitives in Sanity UI. These primitives share many capabilities, although – as their naming and visual treatment suggests – they are intended for different purposes.

```
import declarations may only appear at top level of a module
```

### Layout primitives  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/motivation#layout-primitives)  

Sanity UI features a set of basic building blocks for creating layouts: `Box`, `Container`, `Grid`, `Flex`, `Inline` and `Stack`.

### Interactive primitives  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/motivation#interactive-primitives)  

Most apps needs some degree of input and interactive elements (especially so at Sanity). That's why Sanity UI has basic interactive primitives built-in: `Button`, `Checkbox`, `Radio`, `Select`, `Switch`, `TextArea` and `TextInput`.

### Other primitives  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/motivation#other-primitives)  

Sanity UI also comes with various other elements, such as `Avatar`, `Badge`, `KBD`, `Popover`, `Spinner` and `Tooltip`.

## Accessibility as constraint  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/motivation#accessibility-as-constraint)  

Instead of visual design working against accessibility, Sanity UI is shaped with accessibility as a design constraint.

### Carefully designed focus rings  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/motivation#carefully-designed-focus-rings)  

Focus rings are helpful for all people, whether you use the keyboard or not. Sanity UI even has theming configuration specifically for focus rings.

### Keyboard navigation  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/motivation#keyboard-navigation)  

Keyboard navigation is another aspect that’s useful whether you have visual impairment or not. It enables both expert users to be even more effective, as well as people who cannot use the mouse.

### Accessible components  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/motivation#accessible-components)  

*   Accessible `Dialog` (follows [WAI-ARIA design pattern](https://www.w3.org/TR/wai-aria-practices-1.1/examples/dialog-modal/dialog.html))
    
*   Accessible `MenuButton` (follows [WAI-ARIA](https://www.w3.org/TR/wai-aria-practices-1.1/examples/menu-button/menu-button-links.html))
    
*   Accessible `Autocomplete` (based on best practices and tested using screen readers)
    

## Theming with JavaScript  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/motivation#theming-with-java-script)  

Sanity uses JavaScript for all things on the client side – schemas, configuration, plugins, apps. With Sanity UI, the interface is also completely defined in JavaScript (including theming and CSS).

## TypeScript support  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/motivation#type-script-support)  

TypeScript adds safety and developer experience features like code hinting.

## Enables pixel-perfection  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/motivation#enables-pixel-perfection)  

As a design system strictly based on constraints, scales and themeable values, Sanity UI provides a solid and nearly breakproof framework for implementing visual design.

## E2E testing  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/motivation#e2-e-testing)  

Test coverage with [Cypress](https://www.cypress.io/), to avoid regressions between releases.

# Sanity UI Figma library

Log in to Figma to view this file

No account? [Create one](https://www.sanity.io/signup)

[](https://www.figma.com/community/file/1317452372825980184)

[Show in Figma Community](https://www.figma.com/community/file/1317452372825980184)

Sanity UI was created to make it possible to design with code, directly in the browser.

The easiest way to experiment with the library is to use Arcade – an online sandbox where you mix and match all its components and see the output in real-time.

If you would like to use design software to prototype your Sanity UI experiences we also have [a fully-featured Figma library](https://www.figma.com/community/file/946703925105795979) containing all the components, icons, typography styles and effects included in the project.

# Sizes in Sanity UI

## Why does Sanity UI use a number-based spacing scale?  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/guide/sizes-in-sanity-ui#why-does-sanity-ui-use-a-number-based-spacing-scale)  

Some UI libraries use spacing keys like `none`, `xs`, `s`, `m`, `l` , `xl`, etc., while Sanity UI uses numeric spacing keys.

For example – this is how you define the spacing/size of a button in [Chakra UI](https://v2.chakra-ui.com/docs/components/button):

```
<Button colorScheme='teal' size='sm'>
  Button
</Button>
```

While in Sanity UI you do:

```
<Button padding={2} tone="primary" text="Button" />
```

These are some advantages of number-based spacing scales:

*   Simpler to learn.
    
*   Shorter keys, faster to use.
    
*   Enables arithmetic like `padding={paddingProp - 1}`
    
*   Enables negative spacing like `margin={-4}`
    

## What do the numbers mean?  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/guide/sizes-in-sanity-ui#what-do-the-numbers-mean)  

The numbers are part of a _spacing scale_.

The spacing scale in Sanity UI is a sequence of 10 numbers – 0 to 9. Each number represent a spacing value:

*   `0 = 0px`
    
*   `1 = 4px`
    
*   `2 = 8px`
    
*   `3 = 12px`
    
*   `4 = 20px`
    
*   `5 = 32px`
    
*   `6 = 52px`
    
*   `7 = 84px`
    
*   `8 = 136px`
    
*   `9 = 220px`
    

Using a fibonacci scale gives us an effective scale for combining different paddings and get balanced and aligned compositions.

![The Fibonacci scale](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/mos42crl/production/00348d367a133a83852ab6a61f233bbb0bd3f9e8-3388x2092.png)

Which means:

```
<Card padding={0}> // no padding
<Card padding={1}> // 4px padding
<Card padding={4}> // 20px padding
<Card padding={6}> // 52px padding
```

and so on.

## Other projects using number-based spacing scales  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/guide/sizes-in-sanity-ui#other-projects-using-number-based-spacing-scales)

The `AvatarStack` component displays a horizontal overlapping list of `Avatar` and `AvatarCounter` components.

```
import declarations may only appear at top level of a module
```

<Avatar

color\="magenta"

initials\="uq"

/>

<Avatar

color\="blue"

initials\="uq"

/>

<Avatar

color\="purple"

src\="https://source.unsplash.com/96x96/?face"

/>

</AvatarStack\>

</Card\>

## Properties  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/primitive/avatar-stack#properties)  

```

```

The maximum number of items to render. If items are hidden, will also display the hidden item count as a separate `AvatarCounter` component.

```
size?: 0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | Array<0 | 1 | 2 | 3>
```

```

```

## Accessibility considerations  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/primitive/button#accessibility-considerations)  

All buttons need an “accessible name”, which is the name that screenreaders, voice control software and other assissistive technologies use to refer to this specific button (and distinguish it from others).

If there's `text` in your button, that usually becomes the accessible name. When you use `<Button>` with just an icon and no `text`, always add your accessible name manually. One method to do that is to use the `aria-label` attribute.

For example, this would be a button that is visually just a “plus” icon, but has an accessible name of “Add content”:

```
 <Button
    fontSize={[2, 2, 3]}
    icon={AddIcon}
    mode="ghost"
    padding={[3, 3, 4]}
    aria-label="Add content"
/>
```

shadow\={1}

tone\="caution"

\>

<Text align\="center" size\={\[2, 2, 3\]}\>

Text in a card with <a href\="#"\>link</a\>

</Text\>

</Card\>

<Card

padding\={\[3, 3, 4\]}

radius\={2}

shadow\={1}

tone\="critical"

\>

<Text align\="center" size\={\[2, 2, 3\]}\>

Text in a card with <a href\="#"\>link</a\>

</Text\>

</Card\>

</Stack\>

</Card\>

# Container

The `Container` component wraps content layout in a defined set of widths.

```
import declarations may only appear at top level of a module
```

<Container width\={0}\>

<Card padding\={4}\>

<Text size\={\[2, 2, 3, 4\]}\>Contained text</Text\>

</Card\>

</Container\>

## Properties  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/primitive/container#properties)  

```
as?: React.ElementType | keyof JSX.IntrinsicElements
```

```
display?: 'none' | 'block' | 'grid' | 'flex' | 'inline-block' | Array<'none' | 'block' | 'grid' | 'flex' | 'inline-block'>
```

```
height?: 'stretch' | 'fill' | Array<'stretch' | 'fill'>
```

```
overflow?: 'visible' | 'hidden' | 'auto' | Array<'visible' | 'hidden' | 'auto'>
```

```
sizing?: 'content' | 'border'
```

```
width?: number | number[]
```

# Flex | Sanity UI

<Flex padding\={4}\>

<Card flex\={1}\>

<Text size\={\[2, 2, 3, 4\]}\>hello</Text\>

</Card\>

<Card flex\={\[1, 2, 3\]} marginLeft\={\[2, 2, 3, 4\]}\>

<Text size\={\[2, 2, 3, 4\]}\>world</Text\>

</Card\>

</Flex\>

# Grid | Sanity UI

The `Grid` component is for building 2-dimensional layers (based on CSS grid).

```
import declarations may only appear at top level of a module
```

## Properties  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/primitive/grid#properties)  

```
autoRows?: 'auto' | 'min' | 'max' | 'fr'
```

```
autoCols?: 'auto' | 'min' | 'max' | 'fr'
```

```
autoFlow?: 'row' | 'column' | 'row dense' | 'column dense'
```

```
columns?: number | number[]
```

```
gap?: number | number[]
```

```
rows?: number | number[]
```

# Radio

The `<Radio />` component allows the user to select one option from a set.

```
import declarations may only appear at top level of a module
```

<Card padding\={4} style\={{textAlign: 'center'}}\>

<Inline space\={3}\>

<Radio

checked\={value \=== 'a'}

name\="foo"

onChange\={handleChange}

value\="a"

/>

<Radio

checked\={value \=== 'b'}

name\="foo"

onChange\={handleChange}

value\="b"

/>

<Radio

checked\={value \=== 'c'}

name\="foo"

onChange\={handleChange}

value\="c"

/>

</Inline\>

</Card\>

## Properties  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/primitive/radio#properties)  

The `Radio` component’s properties extend all properties of an `<input type="radio" />` element, except `type`.

```
customValidity?: string
```

# Popover

The `Popover` component is used to display some content on top of another.

```
import declarations may only appear at top level of a module
```

<Box padding\={4} style\={{textAlign: 'center'}}\>

<Popover

content\={<Text size\={\[2, 2, 3, 4\]}\>Hello, world</Text\>}

padding\={4}

placement\="top"

portal

open

\>

<Button

mode\="ghost"

padding\={\[3, 3, 4\]}

text\="Reference"

/>

</Popover\>

</Box\>

## Properties  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/primitive/popover#properties)  

```
animate?: boolean
```

Whether the `Popover` should animate in and out.

```
arrow?: boolean
```

```
boundaryElement?: HTMLElement | null
```

```
children?: React.ReactElement
```

```
constrainSize?: boolean
```

```
content?: React.ReactNode
```

```
disabled?: boolean
```

```
open?: boolean
```

```
padding?: number | number[]
```

```
placement?: 'top' | 'top-start' | 'top-end' | 'right' | 'right-start' | 'right-end' | 'left' | 'left-start' | 'left-end' | 'bottom' | 'bottom-start' | 'bottom-end'
```

```
portal?: boolean
```

Whether or not to render the popover in a portal element.

```
preventOverflow?: boolean
```

```
radius?: number | number[]
```

```
referenceElement?: HTMLElement | null
```

```
scheme?: 'dark' | 'light'
```

# Select

The `<Select />` component provides control of options.

Use the `<Select />` component when you have a list of string options that the user may choose from.

```
import declarations may only appear at top level of a module
```

<Card padding\={4}\>

<Stack\>

<Select

fontSize\={\[2, 2, 3, 4\]}

padding\={\[3, 3, 4\]}

space\={\[3, 3, 4\]}

\>

<optgroup label\="Swedish cars"\>

<option\>Saab</option\>

<option\>Volvo</option\>

</optgroup\>

<optgroup label\="Norwegian cars"\>

<option\>Buddy</option\>

<option\>Think</option\>

</optgroup\>

</Select\>

</Stack\>

</Card\>

## Properties  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/primitive/select#properties)  

```
fontSize?: number | number[]
```

```
padding?: number | number[]
```

```
radius?: number | number[]
```

```
space?: number | number[]
```

# Spinner | Sanity UI

Indicate that something is loading for an indeterminate amount of time.

```
import declarations may only appear at top level of a module
```

<Card padding\={4}\>

<Flex justify\="center"\>

<Spinner muted />

</Flex\>

</Card\>

## Properties  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/primitive/spinner#properties)  

```
muted?: boolean
```

```
size?: number | number[]
```

## Examples  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/primitive/spinner#examples)  

### Centered spinner  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/primitive/spinner#centered-spinner)  

```
import declarations may only appear at top level of a module
```

<Card padding\={4}\>

<Flex align\="center" height\="fill" justify\="center"\>

<Spinner muted />

</Flex\>

</Card\>

### Centered with text  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/primitive/spinner#centered-with-text)  

```
import declarations may only appear at top level of a module
```

<Card padding\={4}\>

<Flex

align\="center"

direction\="column"

gap\={3}

height\="fill"

justify\="center"

\>

<Spinner muted />

<Text muted size\={1}\>

Loading some content…

</Text\>

</Flex\>

</Card\>

Or if you want to horizontally align, you can change to `direction="row"`.

```
import declarations may only appear at top level of a module
```

<Card padding\={4}\>

<Flex

align\="center"

direction\="row" // <-

gap\={3}

height\="fill"

justify\="center"

\>

<Spinner muted />

<Text muted size\={1}\>

Loading some content…

</Text\>

</Flex\>

</Card\>

# Switch | Sanity UI

The `Switch` component allows the user to toggle a setting on and off.

```
import declarations may only appear at top level of a module
```

<Card padding\={4} style\={{textAlign: 'center'}}\>

<Inline space\={\[3, 3, 4, 5\]}\>

<Switch checked />

<Switch checked\={false} />

<Switch />

</Inline\>

</Card\>

## Properties  [](https://www.sanity.io/ui/docs/primitive/switch#properties)  

The `Switch` component’s properties extend all properties of an `<input type="checkbox" />` element, except `type`.

```
indeterminate?: boolean
```
